title: feature_created event payload
$comment: Provenance: authored 2026-04-25 in WU 4.2 (feature-intake skill). Fields: feature_title (required, string), involved_repos (required, array), autonomy_default (required, enum), correlation_id (required, FEAT-YYYY-NNNN). The feature_created event type has existed in event.schema.json since the Phase 1 baseline; this per-type schema is additive, preserving the Phase 1 freeze contract.
description: Payload shape for the `feature_created` event emitted by the specs agent when a new feature registry entry is created during feature intake. The payload captures the human-provided inputs (title, repos, autonomy) alongside the minted correlation ID. The correlation ID is duplicated from the envelope for payload self-containment — downstream consumers that index payloads without re-reading the envelope benefit from the redundancy.

feature_title.description: Human-provided title for the feature. Free-form prose; used in the feature registry's Description section header and in downstream issue titles. Not a machine identifier — the correlation ID is the stable reference.

involved_repos.description: Component repositories this feature touches, as `owner/repo` strings. Mirrors the `involved_repos` frontmatter field at creation time.

autonomy_default.description: Feature-level autonomy default chosen by the human at intake. One of `auto`, `review`, or `supervised`.

correlation_id.description: The feature-level correlation ID minted during intake. Matches the envelope `correlation_id` and the registry filename. Duplicated here for payload self-containment.
